summary: AI marketing agents for brief-to-live campaign execution across enterprise content supply chains.
editor_note: Marketing ops has a funny failure mode: the strategy is approved, then execution disappears into tickets. Gradial maps 4 agent jobs across brief, asset execution, generative engine optimization, and journey insights, with customer stories for T-Mobile, AWS, Prudential, and Cisco. Unlike CMS plugins that fix one publishing step, it sits across the content supply chain. The test is whether governance keeps up when agents touch brand risk.

product_evidence.claim: The official homepage says Gradial creates execution velocity from brief to live across the enterprise content supply chain.
The site names agent jobs for content and campaign execution, asset execution, generative engine optimization, and insights and journeys.
Gradial shows enterprise customer stories and logos including T-Mobile, AWS, Prudential, Cisco, Microsoft, Electronic Arts, and Weber.
Axios reports a $65M Series C led by Insight Partners for Gradial AI agents that help brands create marketing campaigns.

market_context.primary_user: Enterprise marketing, digital operations, content supply-chain, and brand governance teams.
market_context.differentiation: Gradial spans brief, production, optimization, and insight workflows instead of limiting AI to copy generation or CMS publishing.
market_context.why_now: Marketing teams are under pressure to launch more personalized campaigns while keeping brand, legal, and channel governance intact.

risks.claim: The risk is that brand governance and approval complexity may limit how much campaign execution agents can own without human review.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.

tags: marketing ops,ai agents,content supply chain,enterprise ai,campaigns
